formula: Subject + modal + Verb (base form, no -s, no -ed, no to)
when_to_use: Use 'should' for advice/recommendations, 'must' for strong obligation or logical certainty, and 'have to' for external obligation or necessity (rules, requirements from outside yourself).
when_to_use_ar: استخدم should للنصيحة، must للالتزام القوي أو التأكد المنطقي، و have to للالتزام الخارجي أو الضرورة (قواعد أو متطلبات من مصدر خارجي).
why_arabic_speakers_struggle: Learners often use these three interchangeably as generic 'should/must' translations, missing the real distinction: 'should' is much softer than 'must'/'have to', and 'must' (internal obligation/certainty) is subtly different from 'have to' (external rule) even though both translate similarly.

examples.en: I must finish this today — it's my own deadline.
I have to wear a uniform at work — it's the rule.
examples.ar: لازم البس يونيفورم في الشغل (قاعدة خارجية)

examples.en: She must be tired — she worked all night.
We have to leave by 8 AM to catch the flight.
examples.ar: لازم نمشي الساعة ٨ الصبح عشان الطيارة (ضرورة خارجية)

common_errors.explanation: Modal verbs (should, must, can, will) are NEVER followed by 'to' — only 'have to' includes 'to' as part of its own fixed phrase.
Modal verbs never take -s, even with he/she/it — they stay in one unchanging form.

common_errors.wrong: I have to be tired — I worked all night. (meant as a guess)
common_errors.correct: I must be tired — I worked all night.
common_errors.explanation: A logical conclusion/guess about a state uses 'must', not 'have to' (which is about obligation, not deduction).

practice_fill_blank.sentence: You ___ see a doctor if you feel sick. (advice)
I ___ wear a helmet — it's the law. (external rule)
He's not answering — he ___ be asleep. (logical guess)
We ___ finish this project by Friday — my boss said so. (external requirement)
You ___ try that new restaurant — it's amazing! (recommendation)

quick_rule: SHOULD = soft advice. MUST = strong personal obligation OR a confident guess/deduction. HAVE TO = obligation coming from an outside rule, person, or requirement. None of the three modal forms (should/must) ever take -s or 'to' afterward.
quick_rule_ar: SHOULD = نصيحة خفيفة. MUST = التزام شخصي قوي أو استنتاج واثق. HAVE TO = التزام من قاعدة أو شخص خارجي.
mnemonic: SHOULD is a soft SHOULDer tap (gentle). MUST is a MUST-do, no excuses. HAVE TO means someone else's rule made you HAVE to.
connect_to_speaking: Practice with your buddy: give one piece of advice (should), state one strong personal commitment (must), and describe one rule at your job/school (have to) — say all three out loud in full sentences.
